title: Improved sheet for the production of improved vent assemblies
abstract: There is provided a sheet comprising a membrane, and a first layer of a cured material, the first layer being provided on a first side of the membrane, the first layer comprises a first array of apertures, wherein the membrane comprises a retracted material and the sheet is configured such that a portion of the sheet comprising one aperture cut from the sheet corresponds to a vent assembly.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A sheet comprising a membrane, and a first layer of a cured material, the first layer being provided on a first side of the membrane, the first layer comprises a first array of apertures, wherein the membrane comprises a retracted material and the sheet is configured such that a portion of the sheet comprising one aperture cut from the sheet corresponds to a vent assembly. 2. The sheet of claim 1 , wherein the sheet comprises a second layer of a cured material and the second layer is provided on a second side of the membrane, the second layer comprises a second array of apertures, each aperture of the first array of apertures forms an aperture pair with an aperture of the second array of apertures and the aperture pair occlude each other such that the membrane is exposed on both the first side and the second side. 3. The sheet of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the sheet comprises at least one support portion, the at least one support portion being a continuous material and does not comprise an aperture. 4. The sheet of any one preceding claim, wherein the sheet comprises a first protective layer provided on the first layer of a cured material. 5. The sheet of any one of claim 2 to claim 4, wherein the sheet comprises a second protective layer provided on the second layer of a cured material. 6. The sheet of claim 4 or claim 5, wherein the first protective layer and/or the second protective layer comprises polyimide, polyetheretherketone (PEEK), polyamideimide (PAI), poly aryl ether ketone (PAEK), polyethersulfone (PES), polyether ketone ketone (PEKK), or polyphenylene sulfide (PPS/PPSU). 7. The sheet of any one preceding claim, wherein the membrane comprises polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), polyparaxylylene (PPX), polyethylene (PE), poly(tetramethyl-p-silphenylenesiloxane) (PTMPS), polyamide 6, polyurethane, thermoplastic polyurethane, polyvinlidene fluoride (PVDF), polypropylene, polyimide, or polyacrylonitrile (PAN) or combinations thereof. 8. The sheet of any one preceding claim, wherein the cured material has an elastic modulus of at least 5.3 x 10 7 Pa at a shear deformation frequency of 1Hz. 9. The sheet of any one preceding claim, wherein the cured material comprises a thermoset adhesive. 10. The sheet of claim 9, wherein the thermoset adhesive comprises a nitrile phenolic adhesive, epoxy adhesive, polymeric adhesive, acrylic adhesive, silicone adhesive, or polyurethane adhesive, or combinations thereof. 11. A method of preparing a sheet of any preceding claim, the method comprising the steps: providing a first layer of a curable adhesive cured material and a membrane; forming a first array of apertures in the first layer; laminating the first layer onto a first side of the membrane to form a laminate; curing the curable adhesive to form a cured material; and applying heat to the laminate to thereby retract the material of the membrane to form the sheet of any preceding claim. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the method further comprises the steps: providing a second layer of cured material; forming a second array of apertures in the second layer; and laminating the second layer onto a second side of the membrane, wherein each aperture of the first array of apertures forms a pair of apertures with an aperture of the second array of apertures and the apertures of each pair apertures substantially overlap. 13. The method of claim 11 or claim 12, wherein a pressure of at least 68.9 kPa (10 psi) to at least 689 kPa (100 psi) is applied to the laminate to form the sheet. 14. The method of any one of claim 11 to claim 13, wherein a pressure of from 68.9 kPa to 1379 kPa is applied to the laminate to form the sheet. 15. The method of any one of claim 11 to claim 14, wherein a temperature of at least 100Â°C is applied to the laminate to form the sheet. 16. The method of any one of claim 11 to claim 15, wherein a temperature of from 100Â°C to 300Â°C is applied to the laminate to form the sheet. 17. The method of any one of claim 11 to claim 16, wherein a protective layer is placed on the first layer of curable material and, where present on the second layer of curable material, prior to the step of curing the curable material. 18. A method of forming a vent assembly, the method comprising the steps: providing the sheet prepared by the method of any one of claim 11 to claim 17; and cutting around a pair of apertures to release a vent assembly. 19. A vent assembly formed by the method of claim 18 comprising a membrane having a solid boundary on at least one side of the membrane, wherein the solid boundary comprises the cured material, wherein the membrane is retracted. 20. The vent assembly of claim 19, wherein the vent assembly has a decreased acoustic insertion loss standard deviation when installed in a device compared to vent assemblies not formed by the method of claim 18 installed in the device. 21 . The vent assembly of claim 20, wherein the vent assembly has a decreased acoustic insertion loss standard deviation, whilst at least maintaining the insertion loss of the vent assembly compared to vent assemblies not formed by the method of claim 18. 22. The vent assembly of claim 19 to claim 21 , wherein the vent assembly has a decreased insertion loss compared to vent assemblies not formed by the method of claim 18. 23. The vent assemblies of claim 21 , wherein the vent assembly has a standard deviation from the mean insertion loss of less than 5 dB as measured at 1 kHz.
